# opnDossier

> opnDossier is a command-line tool that processes OPNsense and pfSense firewall configuration files (`config.xml`) into human-readable documentation and machine-readable exports. It runs security audits against STIG / SANS / custom compliance plugins, identifies dead firewall rules, redacts sensitive values, and works completely offline for air-gapped environments.

opnDossier is built in Go, ships as a single static binary for Linux / macOS / Windows, and exposes a stable public Go API from v1.5. Every structured output format (Markdown, JSON, YAML, HTML, plain text) is generated from the same `pkg/model.CommonDevice` domain model, so automation pipelines see the same data shape regardless of which surface they consume. Unimplemented subsystems emit a stable `"not yet implemented in <device> converter"` ConversionWarning substring rather than silently producing empty output, so agents can distinguish product gaps from missing configuration.

## Documentation

- [For AI Agents & Automation](https://opndossier.com/for-agents/): Aggregated entry point for runtime-invoking agents — links every stable machine-readable interface (auto-generated CLI reference, JSON/YAML output schemas, exit codes, public Go API, configuration surfaces) in one place
- [Getting Started](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/getting-started/): Tutorial walking a first-time operator through parsing, auditing, and exporting a `config.xml`
- [Commands Overview](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/commands/overview/): Narrative reference for every subcommand with use cases and workflows
- [Configuration Reference](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/configuration-reference/): Every CLI flag, environment variable, and config-file key with types, defaults, and precedence rules
- [Device Support Matrix](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/device-support-matrix/): OPNsense vs. pfSense coverage per `CommonDevice` subsystem (authoritative source of what's supported)

## CLI Reference (auto-generated)

- [opnDossier (root)](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier/): Global flags and subcommand index — regenerated from Cobra on every build
- [audit](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_audit/): Run security audit and compliance checks (blue / red modes)
- [convert](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_convert/): Convert `config.xml` to Markdown, JSON, YAML, HTML, or plain text
- [display](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_display/): Render `config.xml` as formatted Markdown in the terminal
- [validate](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_validate/): Check `config.xml` for structural and semantic correctness
- [diff](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_diff/): Compare two OPNsense configuration files
- [sanitize](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_sanitize/): Redact sensitive information from `config.xml` into a new file
- [config](https://opndossier.com/cli/opnDossier_config/): Manage opnDossier configuration (`init`, `show`, `validate`)

## Data Model & Integration

- [Model Reference](https://opndossier.com/data-model/model-reference/): Auto-generated reference for the `pkg/model.CommonDevice` schema (the JSON/YAML output shape)
- [JSON Export Examples](https://opndossier.com/data-model/examples/json-export/): Real `CommonDevice` JSON outputs for common workflows
- [YAML Processing Examples](https://opndossier.com/data-model/examples/yaml-processing/): Consuming YAML exports in automation pipelines
- [API Reference](https://opndossier.com/development/api/): Full internal API documentation for library consumers
- [Public API Contract](https://opndossier.com/development/public-api/): Semver-committed surface and Regime notes from v1.5 onward

## Security & Plugins

- [Third-Party Plugin Security](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/commands/audit/#third-party-plugin-security): Trust model, preflight restrictions, and operator responsibilities for dynamic `.so` plugins loaded via `--plugin-dir` (Linux/macOS/FreeBSD only; no-op on Windows)
- [Plugin Development Guide](https://opndossier.com/development/plugin-development/): How to author compliance plugins (both built-in via `internal/plugins/*` and dynamic via `-buildmode=plugin`)
- [Security Assurance Case](https://opndossier.com/security/security-assurance/): NIST IR 7608-style assurance case with threat model, trust boundaries, and attack-vector coverage

## Development

- [Architecture Overview](https://opndossier.com/development/architecture/): System design, component interactions, and the parser → converter → enrichment pipeline
- [Development Standards](https://opndossier.com/development/standards/): Go coding standards and project structure
- [CLI Output Policy](https://opndossier.com/development/cli-output-policy/): Which streams are used for what — stdout for structured data, stderr for diagnostics, fd-3 reserved
- [Contributing](https://opndossier.com/development/contributing/): PR process, commit conventions, testing expectations
- [Releasing](https://opndossier.com/development/releasing/): How a release is cut, signed, and published

## Compliance & Reference

- [Compliance Standards](https://opndossier.com/compliance-standards/): Built-in STIG, SANS, and firewall-baseline plugin details
- [Firewall Security Controls](https://opndossier.com/firewall-security-controls-reference/): Reference for the `FIREWALL-XXX` control catalog
- [XML Field Reference](https://opndossier.com/xml-field-reference/): OPNsense / pfSense `config.xml` element reference
- [Pipeline v2 Compliance](https://opndossier.com/pipeline-v2-compliance/): How the single-pass `compliance.Plugin.RunChecks` contract works

## Optional

- [Examples](https://opndossier.com/examples/): Complete worked examples (basic documentation, advanced configuration, audit & compliance, automation & scripting, troubleshooting)
- [Common Workflows](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/workflows/): Operator-oriented end-to-end recipes
- [Security Scoring](https://opndossier.com/user-guide/security-scoring/): How opnDossier scores configurations against a baseline
- [About](https://opndossier.com/about/): Project overview and features
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier): Source code, issue tracker, releases
- [GitHub Action](https://github.com/EvilBit-Labs/opnDossier/blob/main/action.yaml): Pre-built GitHub Action wrapping the CLI for CI-driven audit pipelines
